title: rubus × exacutus
intro: Rubus × exacutus, also known as the cut-leaved blackberry or cut-leaved bramble, is a hybrid of the Rubus fruticosus and Rubus idaeus species, characterized by its distinctive cut leaves and edible fruits. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus × exacutus include its ability to support immune function, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. Rubus × exacutus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin to reap its medicinal benefits.
